input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

### 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check what's available
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
# Install (e.g. .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

### 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

### 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come with VS:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
              --passive --wait"
```

> If you already have VS installed, use `--modify` via the Visual Studio Installer instead.

### 4. Install Java JDK (Android build dependency)

```powershell
# MAUI/Android needs JDK 17
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17
# Verify
java -version
```

### 5. Install Android SDK & accept licenses

```powershell
# The MAUI workload can auto-provision Android pieces:
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true

# --- OR manage manually with cmdline-tools ---
# Discover latest cmdline-tools:
Invoke-RestMethod https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only

# Download & extract (update URL to latest):
$sdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force $sdk
# After extracting cmdline-tools into $sdk\cmdline-tools\latest:

# List available packages dynamically:
& "$sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components:
& "$sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses:
& "$sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

### 6. Set environment variables

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-Command java | Split-Path | Split-Path), "User")
```

### 7. (Optional) Create an Android emulator

```powershell
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it:
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API_35
```

### 8. Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Must run as Administrator
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

### 9. Validate the full setup

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest
cd MauiSmokeTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -p:AdbTarget=-e
```

### 10. Discover versions dynamically (cheat sheet)

```powershell
dotnet --list-sdks                        # installed .NET SDKs
dotnet workload search                    # available workloads
dotnet workload list                      # installed workloads
winget list Microsoft.OpenJDK            # installed JDK
& sdkmanager.bat --list 2>$null |        # available Android SDK packages
  Select-String "platforms;android-"
```

---

**Order matters:** Install .NET SDK → MAUI workload → Visual Studio → JDK → Android SDK → env vars → validate. Steps 1–6 are required; 7–8 are strongly recommended.
